entry.title: SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA - Finding business process improvements with SAP Signavio
entry.content:
As a UX specialist in the SAP S/4HANA Regional Implementation Group, I’m privileged to work with many of our SAP S/4HANA customers. 

As a customer running SAP S/4HANA, you probably fall into one of the following categories:

  • New to SAP S/4HANA and only using SAP GUI.

  • Implemented SAP S/4HANA with some SAP Fiori exposure but not sure you are using the right apps.

  • Implemented SAP S/4HANA and you want to identify areas for improvement.

  • You are aware of new business processes only available in SAP S/4HANA, and you want to find out which SAP Fiori apps to use with these business processes.

Good news! SAP S/4HANA offers LOTS of standard functionality ‘’Out of the box’’ content, including:

  • More than 3K SAP Fiori apps
  • More than 1.5K embedded analytics apps
  • More than 125 Situation Handling templates.
  • New business models only available in SAP Fiori
  • Mix and match more than 10 classic UIs

You can see with significant standard functionality listed above, it can be easy to be unsure as to where to start.

Even more good news! There is now new and improved tooling so you can learn where to start with SAP Fiori and how to identify which business process improvements will bring you new business value.

Here we will introduce the following tools to help implement business process improvements and bring new value to your business:

  • SAP Signavio Process Navigator – find the Best Practice recommendations (SAP Fiori roles and apps) for each business process.
  • SAP Signavio Process Insights - evaluate your current usage based on industry benchmarks to find priority target areas for improvement.

SAP Signavio Process Navigator is a free service that is integrated into SAP for Me to provide access to the following:

SAP Solution Scenarios, such as Lead to Cash or Plan to Fulfil; respective Solution Processes; contextual process information such as integrations, applications, and implemented solution capabilities.

SAP Signavio Process Navigator provides flow diagrams for solution values and solution processes with the following:

  • Detailed solution activities
  • Test scripts (these include which SAP Fiori apps and roles to use with each business process)
  • Setup instructions
  • Localization-specific implementation details
  • Required solution components.
  • Other implementation information

All this helps YOU to achieve tangible value as quickly as possible. Information available in SAP Signavio Process Navigator is structured in line with SAP's Enterprise Architecture methodology providing you with a consumer-grade experience with a modern and intuitive user interface (UI).

You can use the SAP Signavio Process Navigator to assist you in your scoping.  SAP Process Signavio Navigator contains process diagrams and test scripts per Line of Business function that will help you understand:

  • Which SAP Business Roles are relevant to your business processes
  • Which SAP Fiori apps and classic UIs are recommended for your SAP S/4HANA release

Each solution process has a test script with details on BOTH the SAP Fiori roles to use and an overview table of recommended SAP Fiori apps associated with the solution process.

Using SAP Signavio Process Navigator,  YOU can easily find this information as follows, here we will use the Solution Process Accounts Payable (J60) as an example:

  1. Logon to SAP Signavio Process Navigator and click on the Solution Scenarios tile:

  2. Locate the Solution Scenario for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition (Note: This Solution Scenario can  also be used for SAP S/4HANA on-Premise)

  3. Follow the menu path shown Finance->Financial Operations->Accounts Payable (J60). Select the Accounts Payable (J60) link:

  4. From the Accelerators tab, click on the test script and this will download a document (in this case called J60_S4HANA2023_BPD_EN_DE.docx) to your downloads folder

  5. Open the document downloaded in step 4 and view the Table of Contents. Here we can see the following:

    • Roles that contain the SAP Fiori apps for this business process (section 2.2 in this case)
    • An Overview Table of the SAP Fiori apps for this business process (section 3 in this case)

  6. Here in section 2.2, we can see a full list of the roles that contain the SAP Fiori apps for this business process: [Note: Role SAP_BR_AP_ACCOUNTANT is noted here, this will be used in the example in step 8].

  7. Here in section 3, we can see a full list of the roles that contain the SAP Fiori apps for this business process. [Note: SAP Fiori app F1861 Display Supplier List is noted here, this will be used in the example in step 8].

     

  8. With this information, you can review the SAP Fiori app details in the SAP Fiori apps reference library which can be found here: https://url.sap/fiori-apps-library

    The SAP Fiori apps reference library contains detailed information on all SAP Fiori launchpad content for SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud including:

    • SAP Fiori apps
    • Classic Web Dynpro apps
    • Web Client UI
    • Classic SAP GUI transactions

    Based on what you found in the SAP Signavio Process Navigator in step 6 and step 7, proceed as follows using the SAP Fiori apps reference library to search by Role or SAP Fiori App ID.

          a) Search by role: All apps for SAP S/4HANA -> by Roles-> SAP_BR_AP_ACCOUNTANT

           b) Search by SAP Fiori App ID: All apps for SAP S/4HANA ->F1861

NOTE: In some cases you can even view the relevant SAP Fiori apps from the Business Process Model (BPM diagram so you do not even need to look at the test script, this make it VERY EASY to find the SAP Fiori Apps. For example, in SAP Signavio Process Navigator / Solution Scenario / SAP Best Practices for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, you can see directly from the BPM diagram in Solution Process Basic Credit Management (BD6) that SAP Fiori App ID F4596 is used:

 

Conclusion: You now have enough information to plan for and implement the SAP Fiori apps that will bring business process improvements.

SAP Signavio Process Insights is a process analytics solution that helps YOU rapidly discover areas for improvement and automation within your SAP business processes.

What can I achieve with the product?

  • Get a better understanding of your processes.
  • Find the root cause of your problems.
  • Obtain faster value discovery pre-, mid-, or post-transformation.
  • Increase process transparency and process standardization.
  • Reduce transformation time and risks.
  • Start exploring with SAP Signavio Process Insights, discovery edition (see below):

 

  • SAP Signavio Process Insights is the fastest path to process improvements.
  • The SAP Signavio Process Insights solution is a process analytics solution that helps you rapidly discover areas for improvement and automation.

SAP Signavio Process Insights helps you understand your current business process performance and identify new functionalities from SAP S/4HANA, SAP Fiori apps, automation, and intelligent technologies to support your business goals, including:

  • Unique tailored insights into your current operational business process performance, based on data from your SAP ERP or SAP S/4HANA system.
  • Specific insights and recommendations are segmented by line of business and end-to-end processes
  • More functionalities, automation, intelligent technologies, SAP Fiori apps and all the SAP cloud solutions with SAP S/4HANA
  • Build your case for SAP S/4HANA in SAP Signavio Process Insights, discovery edition and secure the buy-in from the business

Refer to SAP Signavio Process Insights, discovery edition now in General Availability!

For release dates for SAP Signavio Process Insights refer to SAP Note 3399700 SAP Signavio Process Insights - Planned Release Dates

Becoming an SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA guru

You’ll find much more on the community topic page for SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA

Other helpful links in the SAP Community:

  • Follow our tag SAP S/4HANA RIG for more from the SAP S/4HANA Customer Care and RIG
  • See all questions and answers about SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA
  • Follow SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA for more blogs and updates
  • Ask a Question about SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA

Brought to you by the SAP S/4HANA Customer Care and RIG.

entry.title: Goods Receipt reversal is not allowed after "Putaway Confirmation". What's next ?
entry.content:
The idea of this blog is to explain one of situations where warehouse clerk has received the stocks by mistake in the system and a goods receipt reversal process is no longer possible due to a system restriction i.e. " Standard GRR is not allowed after putaway confirmation" as described in OSS note - 2551912 - Goods Receipt Reverse not possible by EWM inbound delivery - SAP for Me.

As per standard, if the putaway status is "Completed" for the stock, that means it is putawayed to final bin and it has no delivery reference anymore. Therefore, GRR is not possible further. 

Solution - 

You must create a return delivery in this case. As per system, it interprets that goods have physically arrived at the warehouse however, they need to be returned now. 

In ECC or S/4HANA system, go to Tcode->VL60(Inbound Delivery Processing)->Select Quantity Differences->Enter return quantities to create a "Return Delivery". Delivery then passed over to EWM. you can then pick the corresponding stock for outbound delivery order and perform PGI. 

Pre-requisites to enable a return delivery for quantities in ECC - 

1. A quantity difference profile is created and assigned for the confirmation control key used in the PO. Setting is available in MM->Purchasing->Confirmations->Set Up confirmation Control.

 

2. "via Delivery" indicator must be set for the quantity difference profile. Logistics execution->Services Parts Management->Extended Inbound delivery processing->UI profiles-> Profile:Quantity Differences Shipping Notif./inb. Delivery. 

3. "Delivery Split by warehouse Number" indicator is set for the delivery type used in confirmation. 

I hope the information that i have shared will be helpful. 

Swadhinata Mudali 

Business Process Consultant

Digital Supply Chain Management -Cloud ERP Team

entry.title: SAP enablement service for NIS2 Cybersecurity
entry.content:
NIS2 stands for the most expansive Cybersecurity directive in Europe to date and regardless if you RISE or GROW with SAP, Cybersecurity remains critical and satisfying regulations a challenge.

  • Learn how to navigate current security measures in your SAP landscape and to identify potential gaps.
  • Obtain guidance on aligning Cybersecurity measures with this new regulation and how to implement standards-based Cybersecurity management.

 

 

 

Key Takeaways

  • The EU's NIS2 Directive is a revised version of the existing Network and Information Security (NIS) Directive. It was officially signed and published in December 2022. The "Gesetz zur Umsetzung von EU NIS2 und Stärkung der Cybersicherheit" (NIS2UmsuCG) transposes the directive into German regulation, getting effective October 2024.
  • The NIS2 Directive expands the scope and sharpens the requirements for Cybersecurity through structured requirements for security processes, technical and organizational measures, and reporting obligations.
  • The NIS2 Directive introduces stricter penalties for violations.
  • The implementation of an Information Security Management System (ISMS) facilitates the execution of the NIS2 Directive.
  • SAP supports its customers in preparing for the NIS2 Compliance of their SAP systems and cybersecurity processes with dedicated consulting services.

 

EU NIS2 – Network and Information Security in the European Union

NIS2 is designed to address the shortcomings of its predecessor, NIS, particularly in terms of discrepancies among member states regarding the implementation and requirements of cybersecurity measures. This updated directive has a broader reach and enhances NIS.

The directive aims to protect important sectors and services from Cybersecurity threats. Institutions that meet the thresholds defined in the directive, such as electricity and water plants or banks, must comply with legal requirements to make their systems and networks more secure. The European Union (EU) Member States must transpose the NIS2 directive into national law. In Germany, the directive is transposed into German regulation via the "Gesetz zur Umsetzung von EU NIS2 und Stärkung der Cybersicherheit" (NIS2UmsuCG)“, effective October 18th, 2024.

In short, NIS2 is responsible for the following key modifications:

  • The applicability scope has been extended to incorporate sectors such as telecommunications, social media platforms, public administration, and the food sector. NIS2 also includes subcontractors with access to critical infrastructure, recognizing the significant implications of infrastructure threats that could potentially compromise the security of an entire organization.
  • Introduction of a size cap, whereby medium to large entities (with over 50 employees and an annual turnover exceeding €10 million) in the relevant sectors will be subject to NIS2.
  • An increase in sanctions to a maximum of 10 million EURO or 2% of the total annual global turnover, mirroring the level of GDPR fines. 

 

 

The NIS2 directive impacts approximately 160.000 organizations in the EU due to the expansion in the number of sectors defined by the directive. NIS2 differentiates between "essential" and "important" sectors:

For the first time, NIS2 introduces the concept of "important facilities", broadening the scope of the critical infrastructure. As a result, companies in sectors such as digital marketplaces or the food industry now also fall under the NIS2 directive. In Germany, the legislature estimates about 30,000 companies will be affected by NIS2, with "important institutions" making up over 20,000 of these companies.

 

Challenges for Businesses

NIS2 heightens the requirements for companies. There are four main areas to comply and to ensure security on an organizational level:

  • Risk Management: Organizations need to take measures to minimize cyber risks. It includes enhanced network security, incident management, strong access control, and encryption.
  • Corporate Accountability: Management must oversee, approve, and be prepared to address cyber risks when needed. Breaches might result in different penalties for management, which can potentially become a temporary ban from management roles.
  • Reporting Obligations: Essential and important entities must have processes to report security incidents with a significant impact on their services. NIS2 can set notification deadlines: For instance, initial threat notifications must be made within 24 hours of identifying an incident, with further updates required within 72 hours.
  • Business Continuity: Organizations must have a plan to show how they ensure business continuity in case of major cyber incidents. It is important to include considerations about system recovery, emergency procedures, and having a crisis response team.

 

NIS2 minimum measures to address Cybersecurity threads

Beyond the four primary areas of requirement, NIS2 mandates that essential and important entities establish baseline security measures to counter potential types of cyberthreats. The following are among these:

  • Risk management procedures and security policies for information systems.
  • Policies and procedures for evaluating the effectiveness of security measures as well as policies and procedures for the use of cryptography.
  • A strategy for managing and mitigating security incidents and for managing business operations during and after a security incident.
  • Supply chain security and company-supplier relations require tailored security measures for each supplier, followed by an overall security assessment.
  • Cybersecurity training and a practice for basic computer hygiene.
  • Security measures around the acquisition, development, and operation of systems. It also necessitates having procedures for handling and reporting vulnerabilities.
  • Security procedures for employee access to confidential or critical information must be established, which includes data access policies. It's also essential for concerned organizations to maintain an inventory of all pertinent assets, ensuring their appropriate use and management.
  • The use of multi-factor authentication, continuous authentication solutions, voice, video, and text encryption, along with secure internal emergency communication, when appropriate.

 

SAP enablement service for NIS2 Cybersecurity

SAP offers a SAP Store Service to interactively delve into the potential impacts of NIS2 requirements on SAP landscapes, utilizing S/4HANA as an illustrative case study. During this interactive session we present our viewpoints on Cybersecurity Compliance:

 

Cybersecurity Architecture point of view

The objective to secure business processes in your SAP landscape end-to-end, leveraging all relevant capabilities of SAP’s Cybersecurity and Compliance solutions and services.

 

Cybersecurity Compliance & Assurance point of view 

The need to adhere to Cybersecurity regulations, hence to rely on standards-based security and control management as well as auditing and monitoring your SAP landscape.

 

Cybersecurity Assessment point of view

The desire for detailed insights into the Cybersecurity as-is situation, induced by uncertainty surrounding the current measures in the SAP landscape and the fear of "open flanks".

 

A service available in SAP Store

Find out what EU NIS2 Article 21.2 (i) has to do with ABAP profile parameter rfc/selftrust and learn more about the related SAP Customer Success service offering in SAP Store: https://store.sap.com/dcp/en/

 

About the Author
Oliver Derksen, CISA, M.A. Risk and Compliance Management
Principal Consultant Information Security Management and Integrated Assurance
Success Delivery Center DTS Cybersecurity & Compliance | cscm4s-service@sap.com

entry.title: 10 Steps to Clean Core for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition for Customers
entry.content:
Upcoming bootcamp schedule:


Figure 1: Schedule of upcoming bootcamps

One of the hottest new topics keeping our customers busy during the last few months is how to work towards Clean Core principles.

By doing so there are many benefits to be gained:

  • Achieving higher agility and flexibility.
  • Gaining the ability to leverage the latest SAP innovations.
  • Realizing efficient and more secure operations.
  • Achieving higher data consistency and accuracy.
  • Reducing overall costs including those for development, operations, and upgrades.

Sometimes Clean Core is not fully understood as it is not sufficient to only look at how to create extensions in a Clean Core compliant manner. It is very important to also look at other dimensions. Which are those dimensions?


Figure 2: Clean Core dimensions

Your next question is probably what methodology and tools does SAP offer to strengthen these principles and adapt your system accordingly?

Our SAP S/4HANA Customer Care and Regional Implementation Group (RIG) team has been involved in more than 1,300 SAP S/4HANA implementations over the last years – and most recently to the biggest extent with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition projects.

We have seen and worked on the pain points our customers are facing. Many of such pain points can be mitigated by working towards a Clean Core. We have had many discussions about Clean Core with our customers and implementation partners and have decided to make our learnings and recommendations available to you as well.

During the last few months, we have put together a bootcamp to make you understand the Clean Core dimensions in more detail, including information about available tools, recommended methodology and guidelines that should ideally be followed. We have also included demos and exercises to make it easier to understand why working towards Clean Core is important and what benefits you can gain from it.

We offer it as a new bootcamp at no additional cost called “10 Steps to Clean Core for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition for Customers”.

You will get to learn about:

  • Understanding which areas are affected by Clean Core: Business Processes, Extensibility, Data, Integration, Operations.
  • Watching demos of our tooling, such as: SAP Cloud ALM, SAP Signavio, ABAP Cloud, side-by-side extensions with SAP BTP, etc.
  • Understanding how Clean Core matters for your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition implementation and what benefits you can reap from it.
  • Gaining knowledge about what to pay attention to during SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition upgrades.
  • All following our RISE with SAP methodology.

The bootcamp is offered by our SAP S/4HANA Regional Implementation Group (SAP S/4HANA RIG). The team consists of SAP S/4HANA product experts who have hands-on experience and have been supporting customers moving to SAP S/4HANA since 2015.

The bootcamp is offered for SAP customers at no additional cost and is targeted at different roles within your company. You are welcome to attend all 10 Steps at 2 hours each, but you could also attend the sessions as defined and targeted to your work profiles:

  • Enterprise architect
  • Project manager
  • Basis expert
  • Developer
  • Integration expert
  • Functional expert

The sessions will be guided by experts from the SAP S/4HANA RIG and we encourage open discussion about your challenges and how to overcome them.

If your organization is interested, please send your company name, company’s SAP Customer ERP Number, the names and areas of expertise of up to six members who would be participating in the bootcamp from your company.

Please click here to access the program information document (program flyer). To register, please send an email to S4H_Customer_Care@sap.com .

The bootcamp sessions and target groups are shown below.


Figure 3: Sessions Overview

I’m looking forward to your feedback and ideas you may have in the comments section below. Thank you!

Brought to you by the SAP S/4HANA Customer Care and RIG team.

entry.title: Get Ready for Sapphire 2024 with Three New SAP Business Technology Platform Use Cases
entry.content:
Can you believe it? It’s already time for SAP Sapphire 2024, happening in Orlando and Barcelona, as well as virtually. Join us for days of inspiration, innovation, and insight into the latest trends and technologies at SAP shaping the future of business.

 

SAP Business Technology Platform at SAPPHIRE

In this blog, I'll explore new use cases for 2024, focusing on SAP's key themes. We'll see how SAP Business Technology Platform, powered by Generative AI, can transform the value derived from other SAP business applications. These use cases come with their respective Discovery Center Missions and Business Accelerators, making your journey with SAP BTP both easy and efficient.

If you're heading to the in-person event in Orlando, make sure to catch our session, "Explore Business Scenarios Enabled by SAP BTP That You Can Implement Today." My colleague Denise Ma and I will be sharing practical tips and insights to help you jump-start your SAP BTP journey. It's a must-add to your agenda! Don't forget to stop by the SAP BTP: For Business Applications demo station, where our team will be ready to answer all your questions and help you get the most out of SAP BTP. Also, check out the following sessions by the team:

    • BTP108 | Transforming the future of Fresenius: RISE with SAP and SAP Analytics Cloud
    • BTP110 | Using cross-departmental data for analysis and planning with SAP S/4HANA
    • BTP111 | Driving the data and analytics strategy with SAP software at Lundin Mining
    • BTP154 | Enhance your investment in SAP S/4HANA Cloud with SAP BTP
    • BTP190 | How Nestle and Nokia accelerated transformation of SAP S/4HANA with SAP BTP

 

New Business Technology Platform Use Cases

In today's fast-moving business world, keeping up isn't enough; you need to be innovative, agile, and efficient. That's where SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) comes in. It helps you extend and personalize SAP applications, seamlessly integrate different systems, and empower your team with AI to streamline processes, improve experiences, and make confident decisions. Let's check out three exciting new SAP BTP use cases for 2024:

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Use Case #1. Improve operations and decision-making with the power of Generative AI 

You: You're at a critical crossroads in deciding whether to prioritize modernizing your current landscape of business and processes or pivot towards AI-driven innovations. As per McKinsey’s 2023 survey*, Generative AI is already being regularly used at work among 22 percent of the survey respondents, and Generative AI is expected to significantly disrupt industry competition within three years. However, the risks and complexities involved in leveraging AI based on your business data are preventing you from making the jump. These include the challenges of integrating AI with third-party providers and existing systems, especially when the data involved is sensitive, raising concerns about accuracy and reliability.

How SAP BTP can help: Now is the ideal time to embrace AI, thoughtfully navigate these challenges, and realize the transformative potential it holds for your business. SAP BTP infuses business-ready, trusted, and user-friendly AI capabilities across the platform, to boost your business’ productivity and build AI solutions with your business-relevant and reliable data. Using AI in BTP can also boost the ROI of your existing SAP BTP investments, together with SAP Business applications and other third-party applications. Additionally, SAP BTP improves security by providing increased visibility and control over your AI usage with easily managed access to frontier LLMs, so your data is handled responsibly. Read more about SAP Business AI here.

Check out these sessions:

Orlando
  • BTP189 | Data in the Age of AI 
  • BTP192 | AI in SAP BTP – Vision and strategy
  • BTP197 | Take a lap through AI in SAP BTP
Barcelona
  • BTP210 | Data in the age of AI: Strategy and innovations
  • BTP218 | Unlocking AI's potential: The crucial role of data
  • BTP229 | ABAP Cloud model and generative AI: A power couple to keep your core clean
Virtual
  • PAR309v | Unlock the potential of your data assets: Data-driven decision-making
  • PAR310v | Transforming business with AI – Unlock value and performance

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Use Case #2. Empower employees with enterprise-wide self-service portals

You: Imagine your work monitor crowded with a patchwork of open applications, each window representing a different task you need to manage. Throughout the day, you find yourself clicking back and forth incessantly, switching between these various programs and interfaces. Your business relies on a mix of SAP and third-party solutions, each with its own unique user interface and experience. Harvard Business Review’s study in 2022** provides an example of a Fortune 500 consumer goods organization, where each person in a single supply-chain interaction has to switch about 350 times between 22 different applications and unique websites. Employees need to be trained on each of these solutions, which makes employee onboarding difficult. Many of them must log in to multiple systems to do their day-to-day work. The myriad of entry points to data across different lines of business only adds complexity to the mix.

How SAP BTP can help: SAP BTP answers the call with SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition. By consolidating tools and offering an intuitive and unified entry point for SAP and 3rd party applications, business processes, and information, SAP Build Work Zone fosters collaboration and efficiency. Role-based access paves the way for streamlined workflows, heightened productivity, and a culture of innovation. Giving employees a simplified user experience and access to multiple systems and processes, enabling them to build interactive workspaces visually to better engage with their teams and external stakeholders, truly unifies employee experience and increases productivity. Read more about SAP Build Work Zone here.

 

Orlando
  • BTP120 | Lockheed Martin: Fostering one employee experience with SAP Build Work Zone
  • BTP131 | What's new with SAP Build Work Zone and the SAP Build Apps tool
  • BTP186 | Transformation at Hatch Ltd. with SAP S/4HANA and SAP Build Work Zone
Barcelona
  • PAR240 | CEPSA ‘HR’evolution: Empowering people, transforming human resources
Virtual
  • HCM108v | Close the skills gap with total workforce management

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Use Case #3. Supercharge developer productivity with SAP Build Code and generative AI

You: You are an SAP developer, and many hours of your day are spent navigating complex application logic and checking data models. After all, IDC’s research*** states that developers spend 60% of their time on operational responsibilities, such as the implementation of automation, code testing and execution, while only 15% of their time is spent on coding. All those development and testing experiences while striving to shorten lead times to market pose significant hurdles for professional developers. Speed and flexibility while securing productivity are difficult to achieve.

How SAP BTP can help: SAP Build Code provides generative AI-based code development with Joule copilot, empowering developers to overcome these challenges. It offers an intuitive, turn-key design time and runtime environment for coding, testing, integrations, and application lifecycle management. Use AI to generate app logic, data models, and test scripts, and experience how SAP Build Code accelerates time-to-value for development projects and supercharges developer productivity. Read more about SAP Build Code here.

 

Orlando
  • BTP117 | Generative AI–based code development from SAP Build Code
  • BTP118 | Discover generative AI-based code development from SAP Build Code
  • BTP119 | Application development with SAP Build and SAP Build Code: Road map review
Barcelona
  • BTP138 | Generative AI–based code development from SAP Build Code
Virtual
  • BTP224v | Extend SAP S/4HANA with SAP Build and SAP Build Code

 

Honourable Mention:

Accelerate Financial Planning & Analysis

You: Working and collaborating across the organization gets overwhelming, as Financial Planning & Analysis is not a one-person or even one-department job; the CEO, the offices of CFO, and the other stakeholders across the business all hold key business data and processes crucial to FP&A, but it seems like each area has their own rules and data in different places. This causes delays in accessing the real-time data, impeding proactive planning and agile decision-making.

How SAP BTP can help: With the power of data integration, visualization, and predictive analytics, SAP Analytics Cloud for Planning serves as a reporting, analytics, and planning tool in one. It allows business users to access current real-time data in a simple and timely manner, eliminating delays in the planning process. Your financial planning projects can be fast-tracked with SAP’s prebuilt business accelerators, and activating predictive analytics with Machine Learning capabilities is as easy as a single click. Read more on SAP’s Financial Planning and Analysis solutions here.

 

To wrap up

SAP Business Technology Platform provides organizations with the tools and capabilities to thrive in the digital age. Through the lens of Generative AI, self-service portals, and developer productivity tools, SAP BTP not only addresses challenges but also unlocks opportunities for growth and innovation. It's not just about staying ahead; it's about leading the charge towards excellence. Now that these three new use cases have piqued your interest, visit SAP Business Technology Platform In Action community page for the full list of SAP BTP Use Cases and other resources. 

With a lineup of industry experts, hands-on workshops, and networking opportunities, Sapphire 2024 is not to be missed. We look forward to seeing you in Orlando, Barcelona, or virtually and sharing more about what SAP Business Technology Platform can do for you and your organization. Get ready to unlock your business's full potential and explore the possibilities of the intelligent enterprise.

 

Additional Resources

  • HCM 102 | Technology strategy and road map for SAP SuccessFactors solutions session to learn more about how SAP BTP works with SAP SuccessFactors.

 

*The state of AI in 2023: Generative AI’s breakout year. (2023, August 1). McKinsey & Company. https://www.mckinsey.com/capabilities/quantumblack/our-insights/the-state-of-ai-in-2023-generative-ais-breakout-year

**Murty, R. N. (2022, November 1). How much time and energy do we waste toggling between applications? Harvard Business Review. https://hbr.org/2022/08/how-much-time-and-energy-do-we-waste-toggling-between-applications

***IDC: Generative AI and turnkey development platforms can improve developer productivity. (n.d.). SAP. https://www.sap.com/canada/documents/2024/01/fe86d503-a57e-0010-bca6-c68f7e60039b.html

entry.title: SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA – Trial system options for Private Cloud and On-Premise
entry.content:
When you are planning to move or upgrade to the latest SAP S/4HANA release, nothing beats getting hands-on experience in a real system.  It’s the perfect opportunity to put your self-enablement learning into practice to explore new processes, new tools, new concepts, and try out the latest key user extensions, and Clean Core development techniques.

However, setting up your own sandbox can be a significant resources/effort/time hurdle, especially when you still skilling up your team. Just like your productive landscape, for a sandbox system you will need to work through installation, activation, configuration, and then add some sample data for your exploration.

Did you know? There is an easy alternative!

A pre-configured and fully activated SAP S/4HANA trial system from SAP Cloud Appliance Library (CAL). These trial systems even come complete with sample data, test users, and demo guides to get you started! 

Hint: You can alternatively use the SAP Fiori configuration only SAP S/4HANA trial system, if you want to use your own sample data.

SAP offers various SAP Cloud Appliance Library (CAL) templates for different purposes. These templates enable you to create your own trial and sandbox systems easily.  With the CAL templates you have full administrative rights either hosted in the cloud on your preferred Cloud Provider (such as AWS, Microsoft Azure or Google Cloud Platform) or even on your own on-premise hardware.

Yes! Trials come with a free trial period, and you can extend your trial if needed.

In this blog post you will learn:

  • How you can use your SAP S/4HANA trial system
  • What you get with the latest SAP S/4HANA trial systems
  • How to obtain a trial system from SAP Cloud Appliance Library 
  • Tips on how to use your trial system
  • Extending your trial system
  • FAQ

Personal note: In the SAP S/4HANA RIG we rely on these trials and we use them with many of our customers for bootcamp trainings, demonstrations, and even for writing blog posts like this one! In the right context, these trial systems are lifesavers!

 

How you can use your SAP S/4HANA trial system

As part of the SAP S/4HANA Customer Care program and Regional Implementation Group (S/4HANA RIG) I use these trial systems all the time, for:

  • Exploring new business roles, apps, features, and tools
  • Explore the latest best practice tooling for creating and managing custom business roles
  • View SAP delivered context-sensitive help, such as Quick Tours, Tutorials, Terminology explanations, Guided Tours and more
  • Creating GIFs, videos, courses
  • Crafting bootcamp exercises for our 5 Steps to Fiori bootcamp
  • Running customer demonstrations for our Customer Care customers
  • Checking what’s underneath SAP Fiori apps and embedded analytics – such as VDMs and CDS Views
  • Exploring key user extension options – i.e. some of the most popular on-stack Clean Core extensions
  • Trying out more advanced Clean Core development techniques, such creating SAP Fiori launchpad plug-ins using embedded ABAP Cloud
  • And of course.... writing blog posts like this one!

These are some of the ways customers and partners currently use SAP S/4HANA fully activated trial system:

  • Preparing your move to SAP S/4HANA – net new or system conversion.
  • Planning your upgrade to a new release – you can create trials for different SAP S/4HANA release and Feature Pack Stack (FPS) combinations
  • Exploration of roles, apps, features, context-sensitive help, administration tooling, and extension techniques
  • Demonstrating processes, apps, features, and extension techniques
  • Upskilling on new best practice techniques and tools – such as coming to our 5Steps2Fiori bootcamp
  • Proof of concept explorations, e.g. to try out intelligent experiences such as Situation Handling.

From a legal perspective, you may wonder what is allowed and what is not allowed in the appliance.

Simply put, the first 30 days of using an appliance are governed by an online Terms & Conditions form that you must accept during the appliance creation process.

These terms prohibit productive usage (including uploading respective data), service provisioning to 3rd parties (eg. for prototyping or trainings), and other cases that go beyond the pure evaluation and trial purpose.

After these first 30 days, you will need contracts with SAP for SAP S/4HANA and for SAP CAL, is essence allowing you to use the appliance as specified in the SAP S/4HANA contract.

IMPORTANT: Even under a regular SAP S/4HANA contract with SAP, we STRONGLY advise to NOT use the appliance as starting point for a productive installation, eg. by using the system or parts of it as development system.  Why? The appliance contains contents and settings that might create trouble in your customer-specific project eg. cross-client coding and configuration, irreversibly activated business functions, functions with a separate license, etc. More details about the Terms & Conditions when using an appliance can be found FAQ for Trial Offering (Appliance Templates).

 

What you get with the latest SAP S/4HANA trial systems

In the CAL you will find appliance templates, which you use to create your trial system appliance(s). 

The latest CAL appliance templates include:

  • the fully activated version on SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS01 and
  • the SAP Fiori configuration only version on SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS01.

SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS01, Fully Activated Appliance

Link to appliance template: SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS01, Fully-Activated Appliance

This appliance template contains SAP S/4HANA 2023 (SP01) with pre-activated SAP Best Practices for SAP S/4HANA core functions, and further scenarios for Service, Master Data Governance (MDG), Portfolio Mgmt. (PPM), Human Capital Management (HCM), Analytics, and more. User access happens via SAP Fiori, SAP GUI, SAP HANA Studio, Windows remote desktop, or the backend operating system for full administrative access.

For more details refer to section What is the content & scope of blog post SAP S/4HANA Fully-Activated Appliance: Create your... - SAP Community

What you get: A trial system with pre-configured Fiori setup and pre-activated SAP Best Practices, further configurations in other functional areas and various demo guides for immediate exploration.

SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS01 (SAP Fiori configuration only)

Link to appliance template: SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS01 (Fiori configuration only)

This appliance template contains a pre-configured and activated SAP S/4HANA Fiori UI in client 100, with prerequisite components activated as per SAP note 3411022 - Composite SAP note: Rapid Activation for SAP Fiori in SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS01. User FIORI_USER is prepared accordingly

This CAL system is prepared (in client 100) with activated admin roles assigned to a user named FIORI_USER, including:

  1. Administrator
  2. Configuration Expert – Business Process Configuration
  3. Analytics Specialist
  4. Business Process Specialist

Benefit to customers and partners: You get a system with pre-configured Fiori setup and FIORI_USER to immediately start exploring. You create your own sample data for exploration, demonstrations, and training.

Timeline / Availability of future CAL trial systems

Usually, the SAP Fiori configuration only release is published shortly after the official Release to Customer (RTC) of the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. Refer to: Planned Appliance Template Updates in 2024 - SAP Community

Usually, the Fully Activated release is published about 8 weeks after the official RTC of the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. Refer to: SAP S/4HANA Fully-Activated Appliance: Create your... - SAP Community

 

How to obtain a trial system from SAP Cloud Appliance library

Go to the https://cal.sap.com and select Explore Our Library to review the available appliance templates.

You can review details of which appliance templates are available for your Cloud Provider.  

Select an appliance template to review their contents and other useful information, for example, by reviewing the linked Getting Started Guide.

To create your own trial system from an appliance template, you will need to login to the CAL site at https://cal.sap.com.

Creating a new user is easy! Refer to Getting Started with the Cloud Appliance Library.

You will also need to create a CAL Account using your Cloud Provider subscription details.  

IMPORTANT: You can have different CAL users responsible for the Account - the Account Owner - versus responsible for your trial system(s) - the Appliance Owner.  This can be useful when Cloud Provider subscription details are centrally managed by your organization. Your Account Owner can control which appliance templates can be selected by an appliance owner when creating an appliance.

Then select Create Appliance to start the simple process to create your own trial system from the appliance template. 

 

You can refer to the following videos with step-by-step examples of creating your appliance on your Cloud Provider:

  • For AWS - SAP Cloud Appliance Library: creating an instance in AWS
  • For Azure - SAP Cloud Appliance Library: Creating an instance in Azure
  • For GCP – SAP Cloud Appliance Library: Create an Appliance in Google Cloud Platform

Tips on how to use your trial system

These tips come from our experiences with using trial systems ourselves and with customers on customer bootcamps.  

  • Accessing your trial
  • Security requirements for accessing your trial system
  • Preparing for demonstrations
  • Managing Cloud Provider costs

Accessing your trial

You can access your trial via the following options:

  1. The provided Remote Desktop option.
  2. Local Host (option of applying your own domain and host name)

The provided Remote Desktop is useful if only 1 or 2 people will use the trial system.

If you intend to use your trial system with a larger team, use the Local Host option. With the local host approach, multiple people can concurrently access your trial system. For example: 

  • Business users can login to your trial system’s SAP Fiori launchpad directly from their web browser
  • Administrators and functional configuration experts can login to SAP GUI using SAP Logon
  • Developers can login to your trial system from ABAP Developer Tools for Ecliipse

IMPORTANT: If using the local host option, you should consider selecting the Static IP address option when creating your appliance.  This ensures the IP address is the same whenever you suspend/resume your CAL appliance.

If you don't want your users to have to map the local hosts file, then you can optionally apply your own fully-qualified domain name (FQDN). This is the most convenient access for a larger group of business users  however, you will usually need the help of your IT organization.

Security Requirements for accessing your Trial System

Your trial system is available on the internet. 

You can find details of the ports and protocols needed along with other security aspects in the Getting Started guide for your appliance - section Security Aspects in Cloud Appliance Library. 

Preparing for Demonstrations

There are many demonstration guides provided for the fully activated appliance trials.  Refer to: SAP S/4HANA Fully-Activated Appliance: Demo Guides

Managing Cloud Provider Costs

You can use the Calculate Costs button to estimate the costs based on your Cloud Provider and Region.

In general, storage costs are minimal, as these are charged per month.

Activation costs are per hour. This means that while they may appear small, if you leave your trial instance constantly active they can quickly add up.

For example, you can calculate estimated costs for using your trial system for a 4 week (20 business day) bootcamp, assuming access for 8 hours per day, as follows:

Total Costs = Storage Costs (for 1 month) + Activation Costs (for 20 days x 8 hours per day)

To minimize costs, you can either:

  • Activate and suspend by schedule as part of setting up your appliance, or
  • Manually suspend and resume to minimize your activation time to only when you need it

Once you have created your appliance, you can Edit your appliance to change between scheduled and manual suspension at any time. 

With Activate and suspend by schedule you nominate when your trial system will be available.  You can nominate a once-off, daily, weekly, or advanced schedule for your time zone.  For example, you can make your trial system available Mondays to Fridays between 08:00 to 17:00. 

With Manually Suspend and Resume, you assign trusted responsible person as an operator to manually suspend your appliance instance when you are not using it, and resume your instance whenever you need it again. You can resume your instance whenever you need to.

When you resume your instance, you can optionally nominate to automatically suspend it a future time in hours, e.g. 4 hours from activation. You can Edit your active instance to adjust shorten/extend the current timeline.

If your instance is currently active and you need to extend the suspension time, you can Edit your instance to update it.

Select the “?” Help icon for your appliance template or instance for details on what else you can control.

 

Extending your trial system

Your free trial period per appliance template starts when you accept the terms and conditions for the appliance template.

If you need to extend your trial system beyond the 30-day free trial subscription, you can use your own SAP product licences or a SAP CAL subscription.   Refer to:  How to Keep an Expiring Trial Appliance in SAP Cloud Appliance Library

Hint: For bootcamps, if you want to create one appliance template briefly as a connection test, you can subscribe to a similar appliance template for an earlier SAP S/4HANA release. This is useful when you need to check your firewall and port settings will permit your team to access your appliance.  You can then subscribe to your target appliance template just before the bootcamp to make the most of the free trial period. 

 

FAQ

What product versions are included in a trial system?

Your trial system includes the relevant components for the selected SAP S/4HANA release and Feature Pack Stack (FPS), including:

  • SAP Screen Personas – for simplifying GUI transactions
  • SAP S/4HANA
  • SAP S/4HANA Foundation
  • SAP Fiori Front-end Server for SAP S/4HANA – i.e. embedded FES
  • SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA – i.e. installation of the SAP Fiori apps
  • ABAP Platform for SAP S/4HANA

 

Becoming a SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA guru

You’ll find much more on our  SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA topic page

Other helpful links:

  • See all questions and answers about SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA 
  • Follow SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA for more blogs and updates 
  • Ask a Question about SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA

Brought to you by the SAP S/4HANA RIG and Customer Care team.

entry.title: Where can I start learning SAP S/4HANA Utilities?
entry.content:
We  recommend to access our Executing Business Processes in SAP S/4HANA Utilities learning journey

Overview
This learning journey, “Executing Business Processes in SAP S/4HANA Utilities,” covers the SAP S/4HANA Utilities. ​This course will be released in several parts/updates in the next months. The first part covers the basic overview of SAP S/4HANA Utilities, introducing you to its primary operations, basic functions, and master data.

In the next releases, the Device and Work Management and Meter Reading will be covered with deeper insight into Energy Data Management, Machine Learning applications, and more. A few chapters cover the specifics of the German energy market, including SAP Market Communication, Master Data Updates, Intelligent Metering, Billing Enhancements, and EEG Billing. Targeted training on Contract Accounting, Convergent Invoicing, and Intelligent Metering will also be provided. This course is designed to offer you a broad overview and practical skills in SAP utilities business processes.​

Learning objectives
After completing this learning journey, a learner will

  • be able to navigate SAP S/4HANA Utilities
  • apply concepts of energy data management and financial accounting,
  • use machine learning applications for meter reading and billing,
  • handle area-specific aspects, especially for the German energy sector.

They will be equipped with skills in contract accounting, convergent invoicing, and intelligent metering. Additionally, they will gain experience in tailoring and using the software's key features for utility industries. 

Pass all the quizzes and receive a digital badge

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area.

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you.

We appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

entry.title: The Crucial Role of Problem-Solving in Managing Reliability in Asset-Intensive Organizations
entry.content:
In the realm of asset-intensive industries, where equipment and machinery are pivotal to operations, ensuring reliability with minimum cost is paramount. At the heart of Managing Reliability lies a fundamental aspect: problem-solving. This critical element is something that everyone does every day in their work. Problem-solving is not merely about fixing issues as they arise. Rather, it encompasses a proactive approach to identify, analyze, and address potential problems before they escalate and are detected as functional failures. The organization's capability to solve problems accurately and within the business context is a key to reliable performance. Failure to understand the cause of a failure inevitably leads to more failures and losses.

One of the primary challenges in asset-intensive organizations is the sheer complexity, interconnectedness, and dependencies of systems. A breakdown in one component can have cascading effects, leading to downtime, increased maintenance costs, and compromised safety. Effective problem-solving acts as a preemptive measure against such scenarios, fostering a culture of continuous improvement and operational resilience.

A robust problem-solving framework involves several key stages and steps. It begins with identifying the problem and defining the problem statement. Once the problem statement is defined, one must prioritize and decide to work on the problem. The next step is thorough analysis, which involves collecting and analyzing data that aids in understanding the root cause of the problem. Based on the data analysis, solutions to mitigate the problem will be developed, and these solutions will be tested against the defined problem statement. Once testing is done, the implementation of the solutions comes next. To close the loop on an enterprise-wide level, it is important to monitor and validate performance improvement to close out the problem and share the learnings. The figure below shows the Problem-Solving methodology cycle.

  1. Identification – Operations, Maintenance, Threat Identification, and Safety Critical can help with problem identification.
  2. Definition – What is the actual expected impact? This question helps to define the problem.
  3. Prioritization & Decision – Quantify the risk and prioritize the problems to decide which problem needs to be addressed first.
  4. Understand the problem – Collect and analyze the data for the next phase. Also, understand the cause of the problem through causal reasoning and causal Learning. Perform FMEA and Value Stream mapping.
  5. Solution Development – Develop solutions to mitigate the problem. This can be in the form of projects, Management of Change (MOC) requests, Engineering Designs, Technical Expertise, and Standards development.
  6. Solution Implementation – Test the solutions against the problem statement and implement the solutions. Implementation involves planning, scheduling, and execution.
  7. Validate and Close – Monitor and validate the expected performance and improvement. Close out the problem and share what you learned within and outside the community for greater benefits and reliability excellence.

Techniques of Problem Solving

  1. Reliability Data Analysis – There is a list of core techniques, both qualitative and quantitative, available to support problem-solving. In terms of data analysis, the set of tools such as Pareto Analysis, Weibull Analysis, Crow-AMSAA, Fault Tree Analysis, Life Cycle Cost Analysis (LCCA), and RAMS modeling are quite useful to process collected data and provide actionable insights.
  2. FMEA – Apply this technique to identify all possible Failure Modes (FM), ways in which something might fail, in a design, a manufacturing or assembly process, or a product or service. This can be applied at the component, equipment, functional systems, and unit or asset level.
  3. Value Stream Mapping – This technique or management method helps visualize the end-to-end business process and identify interfaces, resources, the flow of information, and the time consumed. It also helps to identify opportunities for improvement process times to improve productivity and efficiency. This aids in the identification of opportunities for improvement, including bottlenecks, rework, load-leveling, queue times, reliability, yields, and processing times to improve productivity and efficiency.
  4. Causal Learning and Causal Reasoning – Causal Reasoning are the five WHY questions used in everyday problem-solving situations. Causal Learning provides a way to discover the causes of current performance, learn from the causes, and take action to improve future performance.

Expectations and contributions from multiple collaborative teams involved for Problem Solving

  1. Reliability Department – Staffed with experienced personnel skilled in advanced reliability data analysis and problem-solving techniques, the reliability team runs data analysis to aid the operations and maintenance team in investment decisions, risk mitigation, and bad actors' improvement. It is expected that reliability engineers spend 70% of their time focusing on proactive and long-term activities.
  2. Frontline Operations, Maintenance Staff, and Supervisors – This team encounters problems with the equipment before anyone else. It is expected they know the well-developed basic troubleshooting skills for their equipment and process scope and responsibility. Supervisors are expected to support and develop problem-solving skills continually and own the resolution of the problems frontline operations and maintenance staff encounter. Also, they should know how to access available staff resources to support the problems they encounter and be empowered to engage them when required during any phase of problem-solving. This team helps in day-to-day problem-solving.
  3. Exclusive Problem-Solving Team – There is a sponsor for each team, with extended leadership team members accountable for the team's performance. The team can be physically or virtually located in the same area. The team is resilient and is not affected by past failures. Also, this team remains impartial, delivering unbiased results while maintaining respect for others in the organization. This team keeps other team members involved and updated on the progress. Communication with other teams is the key to success. This team understands the benefit of true collaboration and teamwork without underestimating the importance of meeting as a group regularly.
  4. Operations and Maintenance Team – If the frontline operators need additional support to solve a problem, the multi-discipline is typically the next group to support the frontline team on the shop floor. Team members within this team can self-organize into the troubleshooting skills for their equipment and processes. Team members are expected to have skilled technical expertise for operations and failure modes that can affect the equipment and processes they operate and maintain. They know how to access and consult available subject matter expert resources to support team members on the problem they encounter and are empowered to engage SMEs as and when required.

Problem-solving is one of the key elements in managing reliability. It supports overall reliability management and operationalizing an Asset's reliability strategy. Continuous improvement is the hallmark of effective reliability management, and problem-solving serves as the engine driving this improvement cycle. Organizations that prioritize problem-solving as a core competency empower their teams to tackle challenges head-on, foster a culture of innovation, and ultimately enhance operational reliability in asset-intensive environments.

In conclusion, problem-solving is not just a skill but a strategic imperative for managing reliability in asset-intensive organizations. By embracing a proactive problem-solving mindset, leveraging robust frameworks, fostering collaboration, and embracing innovative technologies, organizations can navigate the complexities of asset management with agility, resilience, and sustained success.

entry.title: Sapphire 2024: Make the Connections That Make the Most of Your Supply Chain
entry.content:
Bring Out Your Best.  A simple statement that holds a powerful truth to it, one that is the pivotal theme to this year’s SAP Sapphire and ASUG Conference.  When people think of their best, what comes to mind?  Perhaps personal success in the form of accolades and rewards; to others, reaching a destination at the end of their journey.  For us here in SAP Supply Chain Management, we want one thing: to break down silos!

Whether we realize it or not, supply chain impacts an entire company, and efficient processes cannot be run through siloed processes with expected success. However, good news – there’s something companies can do about it.

Find out how SAP breaks down those walls to turn supply chains into a strategic, sustainable asset and what you can expect to learn and experience at the Sapphire and ASUG Annual Conference.

Leveraging Artificial Intelligence to Drive Business Innovation

Over the last year, Generative AI and AI have been the hottest topic in board rooms across the world, sparking conversations on the latest innovations and technologies that companies can utilize to promote efficiency and drive out complacency.   AI’s relevancy in supply chain is growing daily, addressing challenges from design to operate, as it helps augment the workforce in warehouses and factories through data-driven tools, empowering people within the supply chain to make more informed and timely decisions.

Technologies, as such, have the power to be a transformative force in organizations’ supply chains.  In its utilization, companies can develop smarter assets and products, driving out process inefficiencies, and connecting real-time information to their equipment and products, allowing for innovative business models.  Attendees can now see this technology in action at Sapphire 2024 at these enticing sessions:

SAP and Google Cloud Technology Accelerates AI-Driven Supply Chains

Navigating Today’s Logistics World with Data and AI

Supercharge Field Service Management Operations with AI

I’m Still Standing: Resilience Built into Your Supply Chain

Expect the unexpected has been a statement that’s rung true through supply chains globally. Disruptions can come in many forms – from geopolitical unrest to a surge in demand – both qualifying as a disturbance to your processes. Companies now need to have the ability to not only sense, identify, or predict these disruptions, but also have the visibility built in to adapt to them, as well. Understanding and pinpointing critical areas of risk and opportunity is key to an agile supply chain, as building resiliency into business processes allows companies to stand the test of time.

Companies now have the ability to see disruptions before they happen and have the systems in place to not only sense it but respond to it.  One of the main ways to drive resiliency throughout your supply chain is having those foundational interconnections between those systems, especially between the functional areas of your business.   Interoperability throughout your ecosystem is key, and Sapphire attendees have the chance to learn more at these exciting sessions:

Expand Your Business with a Risk-Resilient and AI-Enabled Supply Chain

Enhancing Supply Chain Resilience with AI and Freight Visibility

Achieve Supply Chain Resiliency Through Planning Excellence

Let’s Talk: Interoperability Across Your Systems

Scalability at large. Rapid deployment of innovation. Ease of adoption. One may question the connection between these three, and they all come to one word: Cloud. SAP’s Cloud ERP allows companies to work in an interoperable system, letting them work across end-to-end departments and solutions to manage cross-functional business processes. In a sense, when organizations successfully integrate the entire supply chain process – from design, planning, and manufacturing to logistics, maintenance, and service – and then integrate that into the rest of the business via native, in-depth ERP, they can drive differentiation.

In today’s digital age, why is this important?

It allows companies to run as an intelligent enterprise, which, in turns, connects the processes that promote better contextualized decisions.  These vital decisions are not only based on data from the business side, but in conjunction with the organizations’ operational side, as well.  This holistic approach promotes internal connectivity, opening the door for a seamless interconnected ecosystem, where organizations can transform the information without complex systems and multiple providers.   Those attending Sapphire can learn more about driving interoperability in a Cloud based environment at these innovative sessions:

Revolutionizing Supply Chains Using RISE with SAP at BBC

Unleash Your Potential with Enterprise-Wide Adoption of Cloud Solutions

Embarking on a Digital Manufacturing Transformation at King’s Hawaiian

The ability to keep up with the ever-evolving and growing rate of change – for instance, sustainability mandates and regulations – plus leveraging Business AI in the Cloud – in essence, having the embedded technology across all your systems and solutions to leverage real-time data and cutting-edge innovation to grow at scale – can not only change the way companies do business, but allow them to bring out their best.

Companies, organizations, executives, and attendees alike now have the chance to learn more about the cutting-edge technology that’s SAP is bringing to offer.  From AI to resiliency to interoperability, companies can better navigate the challenges they face and rapidly adopt to the evolving digital business environment.  At SAP Sapphire, you’ll see how you build a supply chain that will stand the test of time, shift to business-changing transformation, embed cutting-edge innovation across your departments, and, at the core, bring out your best.

We hope to see you in Orlando at SAP Sapphire & ASUG Annual Conference.  Click here to register.

entry.title: Best practices to follow during DTV implementation
entry.content:
Best practices to follow during DTV implementation

Data Transition Validation or DTV tool can expedite the data validation process by pointing out the differences between source and target system state, hence eases the reconciliation process. Drawing experience from many different customers, we came up with some of the best practices customers can follow for optimum result.

These are some of the best practices which can be followed for maximum benefits.

During the conversion or upgrade planning

  • Understand the scope of the DTV tool early in the project planning phase. Check the blog for more information regarding the tool Business data validation using DTV tool
  • Finalize the test scope, which means finalize the reports which you would like to validate using DTV tool early in the project planning.
  • Finalize the data scope(volumes) that you want to validate, for example how many company codes, GL accounts, Fiscal year etc. should be used during the DTV configuration.
  • Use DTV tool for data validation in each cycle of the project (such as dress rehearsal, performance run cycle etc) and improve the DTV run time (execution time) in each cycle to get optimum effect in the production run.
  • It is worth to mention that initial DTV test set up consume some time and this “estimated time” must be included in the project planning phase.

During DTV System preparation

  • It is recommended to use the latest and greatest version of the DTV tool. More info in SAP Note: 3117879 - Data Transition Validation DTV Tool - Central Note
  • Ensure to implement all DTV related SAP Notes for smooth execution.
  • Utilize the system resources such as executing server and executing group wisely to fine tune the performance of the DTV execution time.
  • Maintain optimal number of background jobs. The reports are executed via background jobs in DTV.
  • Make sure to maintain recommended values for SHMA configuration and memory parameter set up (RZ11)
    • rdisp/PG_KEEP - value = 30
    • rdisp/PG_MAXFS - value = 256000
    • rdisp/PG_SHM - value = 256000
    • abap/shared_objects_size_MB - value = 1800

You can also follow SAP Note 3226794 - SYSTEM_SHM_AREA_OBSOLETE error while using the DTV tool

  • Import the latest pre-delivered content.3258383 - DTV : SAP Pre- delivered Content
  • In the initial cycles, it would be good to have a copy of the source system post DTV source execution for reconciliation purposes later in the project (not mandatory, but recommended)
  • Make use of project export/ import functionality of DTV tool to save time and effort. Once a project is configured in one system, it can be exported and then imported to the subsequent system of the system landscape easily with this functionality.

During DTV configuration

  • For faster extraction and good performance split the data into small chunks to avoid long running jobs. e.g., Create multiple entry for company code in global project data.
  • Import the report multiple times in case of different variant configured for same report.
  • If you are using variant for report execution, make sure it exists in all the systems where DTV runs. 
  • DTV works only for the ALV report outputs. Make sure the variant you choose for report output is not classical list but one of the ALV outputs. For example: RFBILA00, by default has List output. If you use this in variant, then DTV will not result any data.
  • You can create multiple project global data definition for the same parameter. For example, company_code for fixed assets, company code for MM, and have different set of relevant company codes.
  • DTV executes a given report for each combination of values for the split parameter maintained. Hence you can control the granularity/ data volume of the execution by providing appropriate splits.
  • If the same parameter is specified in Split , variant and conditions : DTV will evaluate as mentioned in the help documentation
  • Use Simulation, until you find the right split size. It is not needed to execute Simulation post this.
  • Ensure to define same input parameter for source and target system.
  • Ensure not to change the project global data in target system.
  • Make use of exclude parameter to specify fields that would be excluded from the validation.
  • Custom report with ALV output can also be validated using DTV tool starting DTV 1.4             Business Data Validation of Custom Reports using DTV
  • For custom reports, adjust the output keys in case the evaluation is not happening with the right keys.

During DTV execution

  •  If Simulation is executed for any report, you can use the Import Simulation Results to avoid a   second time extraction. Check the blog for more information on simulation                         Simulation made easy in DTV 1.4
  • The result data shows only 10 K records on load. In case you want to see all the records, click on the refresh button in the ALV toolbar.

Post DTV Execution

  • “DTV is not a reconciliation tool, but is a validation tool”, hence, in case of differences pointed out by the tool, user may consider following below steps as a quick check.
    • Run the report in SE38 with same input parameters.
    • Then, compare the results (obtained from SE38) with the DTV tool results.
    • Reconcile the results in both source (copy of a source system) and target system to ensure the correctness of the tool.

Conclusion

DTV is an easy-to-use yet powerful tool which can help in business data reconciliation by showing the differences between the source and the target system automatically. After a successful Evaluation, DTV can only point out the discrepancies in the Target system (if any) and it is up to the users to perform the reconciliation.

entry.title: FPM Team: Transport of BICS Bookmarks and SEARCH UIBB Views
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

In 2021, the Web Dynpro Multidimensional Data Grid App replaced the SAP Design Studio app as default reporting tool for drill-down reporting in SAP S/4HANA. This app is using the Search UIBB provided by FPM for variable selection.

For the two components Grid and Search used in the Web Dynpro Multidimensional Data Grid it is possible to save the actual state (search View for the variables and Bookmark for the Grid (Layout for the grid and if wanted variables of the search)) in the actual system. These bookmarks and saved searches are stored in the system locally and cannot be transported automatically to other systems.

We from the FPM team, have now made it possible to transfer views and bookmarks from one system to another.

The source and the target system needs to be at last on a special Support Package level (for the saved searches SAP_UI 7.57SP006, SAP_UI 758 SP001 (note 3386445) and Release SAP_BW 757 SP003 for the bookmarks in the BICS Grid note 3336542).

For both components there are reports available which allow the user to collect the bookmarks/Saved searches and write them on a customizing request as well as reports in the target system to import them into the environment.

For the Search UIBB the reports are

  • FPM_SEARCH_TRANSPORT_VIEWS (Source system report)
  • FPM_SEARCH_IMPORT_SSEARCH (Target system report)

The source system report needs to be called within transaction SE38.
The user has to enter a search configuration ID (e.g. FPM_BICS_SEARCH_GENERIC for the BICS Grid standard configuration) for which the public search views should be transported.
The search IDs can be selected from the list as well as the deleted views which should be deleted in the target system.
These views will be written on a customizing request and can be imported into the target system with the target system report after the transport has reached the target system.
This importing report can be started via transaction SE38 and has an option to run a test first.

Please note that your system needs to be configured correctly for customizing request (transaction SCC4)

For the GRID the reports to transport the bookmarks are

  • FPM_BICS_TRANSPORT_BOOKMARKS (Source system report)
  • FPM_BICS_IMPORT_BOOKMARKS (Target system report)

When calling the source system report for the BICS Grid you need to know the query for which the bookmarks should be transported. The name of the query can be found on the query information tab in section Technical Information field “Query Technical Name” in your application.
For the importing report there is test run available and some more options like you have for the search UIBB views.

Now you have learned how to transport saved searches and bookmarks from one system to another.


Please note that the transport of saved searches not only works for variable selection for the BICS Grid but for all Search configurations.

entry.title: SAP Mentor Interview with Chief Marketing Officer for Cloud ERP
entry.content:
Earlier this month I had the opportunity to interview Eric van Rossum, SAP’s Chief Marketing Officer for Cloud ERP. You can watch the video of our discussion and read the highlights below, where you’ll also find related learning materials. Enjoy!

Eric van Rossum is the Chief Marketing Officer for Cloud ERP at SAP, overseeing product marketing for the company's cloud ERP portfolio. He serves as the strategic bridge between product development and customer acquisition, ensuring product messaging and positioning are attuned to customer needs, and facilitating the successful launch and adoption of products in the market. Eric has been with SAP for 24 years and brings a wealth of experience across various functions including consulting, sales, corporate strategy, and support.

To help me summarize the content of the interview, I've used SAP Generative AI Hub :-). Check here what it ays are the highlights of our conversation: 

  • SAP has two cloud ERP offerings: RISE with SAP and GROW with SAP
  • RISE with SAP is targeted toward SAP’s installed-base customers, providing them a path to cloud ERP at their pace.
  • GROW with SAP is targeted toward net new customers to SAP, and installed base customers who want to take a greenfield approach to cloud ERP.
  • SAP is focusing on making ERP an enabler for business model changes, driving sustainability, and improving efficiency, as these are the key customer needs currently in the market.
  • With RISE and GROW, customers should be able to update their software regularly, making them innovation-ready and efficient, especially with the increasing prevalence of AI.
  • Eric believes the shift to the cloud also requires a paradigm shift within companies, citing an example of a customer who moved towards a standardized system and adopted SAP's out-of-the-box solutions.
  • One of the significant hurdles for customers adopting cloud ERP is the organizational readiness to standardize their processes, which requires a significant cultural shift.
  • Moving ERP to the cloud is seen as risky by some companies due to the critical role ERP systems play in company operations. They need strategies to transition without disrupting their business.
  • Eric is interested in a continued dialogue with the SAP Community, to hear from end users and improve SAP’s offerings to meet their needs better.

If you’re going to Sapphire in Orlando or Barcelona, you can hear more from Eric in these sessions:

SAP Sapphire Orlando (June 3-5)

  • Unlock Business Agility with Cloud ERP (Session code: ERP169)

  • Supercharge Your Business and Get Innovation Ready with RISE with SAP (Session code: ERP168)

  • The New ERP Mindset (Session code: TRE321)

SAP Sapphire Barcelona (June 11-13)

  • Unlock Business Agility with Cloud ERP (Session code: ERP146)

If you’re planning to join Sapphire virtual, you can hear more in these sessions:

  • ERP126 | SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition: Discover planned innovations

  • ERP127 | SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition: Find out what is next

  • ERP200v | Accelerate business growth with S/4AHANA Cloud Public Edition

And to learn more about the topics discussed in this interview, the following learning journeys are especially recommended:

  • Exploring SAP Cloud ERP learning journey as a starting point to start learning about SAP Cloud ERP, how to distinguish SAP’s cloud ERP solutions and transformation offerings (GROW & RISE), establish a cloud mindset (fit-to-standard, SAP best practices, integration and extensibility, clean core), and get insights into business AI and sustainability fundamentals
  • Implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition learning journey teaching fundamental implementation knowledge for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition leading to the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition implementation consultant certification complemented by quarterly live sessions, stay certified content and assessments focusing on AI capabilities and intelligent ERP innovations
  • Implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition learning journey teaching implementation fundamentals for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition leading to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition implementation consultant certification, complemented by stay certified content and assessments focusing on how to Manage a Clean Core

Those fundamentals learning journeys are complemented by various line of business specific learning offerings and certifications focusing on business processes, best practices content, and configuration/integration/extensibility options

entry.title: SAP Collaborative ERP adds Share as Card option to SAP S/4HANA Cloud – Microsoft Teams integration
entry.content:
As AI continues to take over more routine functions for knowledge workers, the remaining tasks often fall to experts or teams of experts. So, collaboration becomes even more important so people can complete tasks in real time, rather than delaying critical decisions because of latency in communication. This SAP S/4HANA integration with Microsoft Teams adds another option to SAP’s collaborative ERP, allowing people to share content as a card, in addition to the share as tab or share as chat functions.

With SAP S/4HANA 2402, we added the ability to share apps as a card, based on the collaborative stage view in Microsoft Teams. This allows the recipient to directly edit the data in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition within the Collaborative Stage View of Microsoft Teams. The value is similar to our other share with Microsoft Teams options – allow people to get more done without task shifting.

One example of how this might work is if one colleague asks another to remove a delivery block on an order. This new capability allows them to collaborate via chat in Microsoft Teams while simultaneously interacting with the relevant data in SAP S/4HANA.

Collaborate using the Collaborative Stage View of Microsoft Teams.

 

To summarize the collaborative ERP capabilities in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition that are generally available, we allow Microsoft Teams users to

  • Share as Chat – include a link to the SAP S/4HANA app in a Microsoft Teams chat
  • Share as Tab – display the SAP S/4HANA app in a tab in Microsoft Teams
